cards.front: NOT gate expression and truth table?
cards.back: Z = X̄. Input 0→1, Input 1→0. Inverts/complements the input. One input, one output.
Z = X₀ + X₁. Output 1 when ANY input is 1. Only 0 when ALL inputs are 0.
Z = X₀ · X₁. Output 1 ONLY when ALL inputs are 1. Any 0 input forces output to 0.

cards.front: How does a transistor inverter work?
cards.back: Input 0V → transistor OFF → output pulled to V_CC (5V). Input 5V → transistor ON (saturated) → output ≈ 0V. Implements NOT.

cards.front: How does a diode OR gate work?
cards.back: Each input connects to output through a forward diode. Any HIGH input forward-biases its diode, pulling output HIGH. Output ≈ V_in – 0.7V (diode drop).

cards.front: How does a diode AND gate work?
cards.back: Output pulled HIGH via resistor to V_CC. Each input connected through diode. Any LOW input forward-biases diode, pulling output LOW. All HIGH → no conduction → output stays HIGH.
Using transistors (not just diodes) to restore signal to full rail voltage (0V or 5V) at each gate. Prevents signal degradation through cascaded stages.
Logic 0 = 0V (GND). Logic 1 = 5V (V_CC). Forbidden zone in between. Modern variants: 3.3V, 1.8V logic.
Collector-emitter saturation voltage: ~0.1–0.3V when BJT is fully ON. Close to 0V but not exactly zero. Still registers as logic 0.
Swap 0↔1 in all inputs/outputs: AND becomes OR and vice versa. This is De Morgan's duality and runs through all Boolean algebra.
